name: turmeric
effects: May modestly reduce osteoarthritis pain and stiffness
Anti-inflammatory signal reduction in some trials
Adjunct symptom support rather than disease-modifying treatment
interactions: Potential additive bleeding risk with anticoagulant or antiplatelet medicines.
safetyNotes: Moderate risk: side effects or medication interactions are plausible. Use caution with anticoagulants or antiplatelet drugs.

sources.title: PubMed search: turmeric

description: Turmeric is typically selected for daily wellness routines where users want a familiar food-compatible herb with standardized extract options available.
dosage: Common oral use is about 1-3 g dried rhizome daily or standardized curcumin extracts per label.
duration: Usually framed as a daily consistency herb; reassess tolerance and medication context during longer use periods.
preparation: Common options include cooking powder, capsules, and concentrated extracts with absorption-support ingredients.

categoryUseContext: Frequently compared for long-term daily use, food-first routines, and extract-based convenience formats.
evidenceLevel: Moderate evidence for selected inflammatory-context outcomes in standardized curcumin products; culinary turmeric and extracts are not equivalent.
